title: Backroom Colonists

{{DISPLAYTITLE:Backroom Colonists}}{{CSS|content={{User:1morro.com/BC.css}}}}
"Even during the darkest of nights, stars permeate, and shine bright..."
{{CSS|content={{User:1morro.com/Templates.css}}}}{{Faction Class|hostility=2|influence=high|technology=high|organization=medium|resource=extreme}}
[[File:Backroom colonists.jpg|center|600x600px|link=]]

''Two members of The Backroom Colonists exploring [[Level 10]].''
== Description == The Backroom Colonists, formerly known as the Backroom Pioneers and sometimes referred to as the Backroom Explorers, is a colony initially comprised of various factions and smaller groups that ultimately unified under an elected minister. Despite setbacks, this faction still stands as the largest singular group within the known backrooms and ranks among the most powerful and influential groups, second only to the [[M.E.G]] and thriving across hundreds of levels. While being a united group, each member retains significant freedom and control over their own actions. This autonomy plays a pivotal role in the growth of the Backroom Colonists, as joining is straightforward, and the organization only exercises loose authority over its outposts. Although calculating the total population is quite challenging, given that members are dispersed across numerous outposts on different levels, with the loose affiliation fostering more individuals to constantly join and leave, estimates still put their overall numbers far above second and third place, that being the [[M.E.G]] and [[Aurielle]]. What sets them back however, is their loose organization, while allowing the group to be more appealing and welcoming, has drastically lowered their efficiency. It is worth noting that the Backroom Colonists were once more formidable. However, conflicts and internal reforms led to the substantial weakening of the colony. Numerous outposts and bases were either scaled back, abandoned, or left for other groups to claim. Divisions broke away from the Backroom Colonists, vanishing into obscurity. Since those events, the Backroom Colonists have dispersed, engaged in various conflicts, and endured through multiple periods within the Backrooms. == Political Status == The Backroom Colonists are welcoming to regular wanderers and are willing to provide a certain amount of supplements and medical assistance to all peaceful individuals. Unlike factions such as the [[M.E.G.]] and [[The Kauer Research Organization]], which assign specific roles upon joining, the Backroom Colonists offer a more flexible experience. Wanderers are allowed to gather resources and live under the protection of their outpost, functioning more like a close-knit commune rather than an uncompromising contingent. Positions such as researchers or security personnel are usually volunteered; although it is sometimes encouraged if said member has prior experience or has shown innate talent in a certain field. Currently, the Backroom Colonists maintain friendly relations with most colonies. A comprehensive list of their political relations with notable factions is provided below. |-| M.E.G. = === Description === The relationship between the M.E.G. and the Backroom Colonists is a complex tapestry of alliance, conflict, and eventual reconciliation. It began with a foundation of mutual interest, leading to the formation of the Allied Liminal Coalition—a powerful partnership that successfully defeated the common threat of the U.E.C. in the Great Wake War. However, as both groups expanded, trust eroded into suspicion and competition, resulting in active sabotage on both sides. This, along with various other factors, led both factions to become major participants in the protracted and bitter conflict known as the Bloodbath, from which the M.E.G. ultimately emerged victorious. Through negotiations involving technology sharing and demilitarization, the Colonists were permitted to reform. However, their continued support for Aureille—a faction opposed by the M.E.G. during the Korthie Casiagen War—further strains relations. The aftermath resulted in a tense stalemate, but shared challenges—along with the existential crisis posed by the Flicker—compelled both groups to return to cooperation. From this necessity, a new, pragmatic partnership has developed. Today, the two groups maintain exceptionally close ties, characterized by joint holdings, integrated trade, and unprecedented technological collaboration, symbolizing a hard-won peace built upon their shared history. |-| K.R.O. = === Description === The Kauer-Colonist Alliance was officially established in the Treaty of Fikes. This alliance is primarily pragmatic, creating a formal, albeit limited, framework for cooperation. While the core objectives of the two groups are largely disparate, they are prepared to support one another. Despite having few commonalities, the Colonists have provided protection, traded resources, and occasionally shared research. Both factions have also participated in documenting [[Level 24]]. |-| A.E.G. = === Description === On the surface, the Colonists and the Anti-Entity Group (A.E.G.) appear to have a simple, neutral-to-friendly relationship, with the A.E.G. periodically commissioning the Colonists to conduct census counts, as their large population provides the necessary experience and infrastructure to carry out these tasks efficiently. However, any efforts to deepen their relationship are fundamentally hindered by a core philosophical divide: the A.E.G. is dedicated to the eradication of entities and believes in human superiority, while the Colonists maintain a very liberal and pro-entity-friendly perspective, a stance that leads some Colonists to view the A.E.G. as potential fascists and extremists. |-| E.R.C. = === Description === The Colonists and the Entity Research Committee (E.R.C.) maintain a cooperative, though narrowly defined, relationship. Despite differing objectives, their shared presence on [[Level 5]] has led to close collaboration within the level’s outposts, with the Colonist documenting the environmental aspects while the E.R.C. catalogs potential local entities—including the alleged previous abundance of [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] and the speculated [[Entity 18|Beast of Level 5]]. Additionally, both groups have shared a variety of research documents, but interaction beyond this between the two groups remains limited. Lando Carlsen, the Colonists' head of diplomacy, however, has expressed interest in strengthening relationships, indicating the potential for a more substantial alliance in the future. |-| Aurielle = === Description === The Backroom Colonists share a strong and enduring alliance with the Republic of Aurielle, a bond forged through shared history. The Colonists have acted to protect splinter Auriellen factions such as the Istauran Federation and provided critical humanitarian and material aid to Aurielle during the Korthie Casiagen War. This partnership culminated in the creation of the Geminia Divisional Group, an autonomous unit of Aurielle volunteers integrated into the Colonists. Today, the alliance remains strong, characterized by joint research, military exercises, and fast-tracked access between their respective holdings. In stark contrast, the Colonists hold an openly hostile stance toward the Emporium of Agoreas—a criminal syndicate also known as the Agorean Mafia, which operates covertly within Aurielle’s ranks. The Colonists became aware of Agoreas through intercepted intelligence indicating their illicit drug shipments were being concealed within legitimate cargo, potentially compromising Colonist supply lines. While no formal diplomatic engagement exists, the prevailing sentiment is one of deep opposition, with many advocating for open warfare with the syndicate. |-| Backrooms Red Cross = === Description === The Backroom Colonists maintain a formal and enduring humanitarian partnership with the Backrooms Red Cross, founded upon a shared commitment to aiding wanderers throughout the Backrooms. The Red Cross has consistently served as an integral medical backbone for the Colonists, providing critical care during crises and alleviating strain on the Department of Welfare. This cooperation deepened significantly during the widespread catastrophe of the Flicker, leading to the establishment of temporary joint medical facilities in holdings such as Base Blink. Recently, the alliance has expanded into collaborative medical research, with both organizations pooling knowledge and resources in pursuit of cures for entity-borne pathologies, most notably the [[Sanguine Festivus]] Virus. |-| Party Crashers = === Description === Although not the closest in diplomacy, Party Crashers and Backrooms Colonists maintain cordial, neutral, and mutually respectful relations, with both factions honoring established territorial boundaries. Relations were strengthened following the Colonists' recognized contributions during the Euphoric Wars, leading to further partnerships between the two groups. This cooperative dynamic has enabled crasher units to be stationed across seven different Backrooms Colonist bases. |-| Republic of 153 = === Description === The Colonists and the Republic of [[Level 153]] enjoy a strong and positive relationship. Since the establishment of Outpost Vintage, the Republic has actively participated in trade with the Colonists. During the events of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]], the Colonists did their best to help the Republic avoid mass starvation. Additionally, the group recognizes Colonist Mina as an official and interchangeable currency. |-| R.E.U. = === Description === The Backroom Colonists maintain friendly and constructive relations with the Royal Exploration Unit (R.E.U.). The two groups share significant historical and cultural affinities, which have fostered a generally friendly and cooperative dynamic. While the R.E.U.’s existing alliance with the M.E.G. initially complicated diplomacy, this tension resolved as Colonist–M.E.G. relations improved in the wake of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]]. Though the Colonists and the R.E.U. possess broadly similar exploratory and settlement-oriented goals, they operate under a clear understanding of non-interference, ensuring their activities remain complementary rather than competitive. This mutual respect has allowed for the establishment of beneficial trade agreements and resource exchanges, which have flourished over time and contributed to the prosperity and operational capacity of both organizations. == Hierarchy == === Structure === {{Blurimage|file=Backroom colonist flag V2.png|caption=The current flag representing The Backroom Colonists.}} The governmental structure of the Colonists is closely tied to the roles of the minister, heads of departments, and Arche Parliament (formerly the founding cabinet). The minister, who is elected every two capital years, acts as highest executive authority of the Backroom Colonists, responsible for implementing and enforcing laws while appointing and collaborating with the heads of departments (also referred to as the overseers); overseers are responsible for security, regulation, development, exploration, relations, and welfare of the Backroom Colonists respectively, while providing support and directive to outposts. Outpost leaders are the authority below the heads of departments and hold the leadership of that specific outpost. The Backroom Colonists are very flexible with what an outpost leader has to do. Aside from abiding by human rights and a few other policies, the outpost can operate independently, since the heads of departments will not interfere unless necessary. The Arche Parliament is responsible for processing civilian complaints and forming constitution, functioning as the Colonist's central deliberative body. However, with significant lawmaking power delegated to individual outposts, the Parliament often focuses on broad suggestions and overall quality-of-life improvements rather than direct top-down legislation. Its defining feature is a fusion of powers: elected members double as legislators to an adjudicatory council, judging cases as a council of juries. Their discussions are guided by the Objective Normative Ensemble (O.N.E.), an autonomous logic model that acts as a procedural guardian and arbitral framework, ensuring debates remain rooted in foundational law and logical consistency. For major crimes, this system can expand to include direct community voting. Ultimately, this forms a flexible governance model that thrives on adaptive debate but demands high civic engagement; the downside of this system lies in its chaotic and inefficient nature. === Decision making === While each outpost may have different ways of regulation, small decisions from specific outposts are usually decided together as a team, although the leader would hold more influence. However, big decisions will be submitted to the minister/heads of departments and discussed with the majority as a whole. === Departments ===
====== Department of Security ====== Covers general security and military operations, being staffed mostly by security/logistics personnel and strategists. Furthermore, this department is responsible for civil justice and protection, commonly working with the Department of Regulation. The current head of this department is Darren Nakamura. ====== Department of Administration ====== Covers secretaries, confidants, and small registered groups that control entities, while also supervising the formation and regulation of outposts. This department also collaborates closely with the Department of Security in civil justice, providing necessary statistical reports. The current head of this department is Lee Hollingsworth. ====== Department of Development ====== Covers the economic development of outposts and manages the businesses operated by the Backroom Colonists. It is primarily composed of financial advisors and accountants. Additionally, this branch allocates funds to the outposts and supports the Colonists as a whole for development and scientific research. The current head of this department is Mitchell Texter. ====== Department of Exploration ====== Covers dispatch/explorer groups who discover new levels and scavenge resources native to the level or generated through [[Phenomenon 7]]. Currently, one of their primary objective is to locate any lost Backroom Colonists personnel from before the reformation or any other abandoned outposts. The current head of this department is Laurel Yew. ====== Department of Relations ====== Covers both internal and external political affairs, primarily staffed by ambassadors and advisors. Its focus is on researching and understanding diplomatic protocols while staying informed about foreign policy, establishing and maintaining relationships with other factions, negotiating treaties, and addressing conflicts. The current head of this department is Lando Carlsen. ====== Department of Welfare ====== Covers essential services to ensure an adequate supply of water, resources, and utilities across various outposts. Furthermore, the department is involved in research on treatments and medicine production with the Department of Development. Recently, their focus has shifted to finding cures for diseases caused by common entity pathogens, such as the Sanguine Festivus Virus. The current head of this department is Juno Chilperic.
== Autonomous divisions == Autonomous divisions are segments of the Colonists that function as semi-independent municipalities, each with its own system of outposts, regulations, and governance structure. Some of these divisions may even have unique insignia and vibrant foreign cultures. The purpose of this system is to accommodate previously independent factions that are considering merging while still wishing to maintain a degree of autonomy, or to assist colonies that need to repay debts. These municipalities are typically established through extensive negotiations, with contracts tailored to create mutually beneficial relationships, differing from one autonomous division to another. While previous autonomous divisions such as the Colony of Geminia and remnants of the Aurielle's Istauran splinter government existed, many of these were temporary and eventually merged entirely with or departed from the Backroom Colonists. At present, the Colonists operate five distinct autonomous divisions. |-| Azure Collective = === Status: Active === The Azure Collective is a semi-nomadic wanderer assistance group within the Main Nine. It has its own independent militia, diplomatic relations, and leader. Established after the Korthie Casiagen War, the Azure Collective was the first divisional group formed in the post-Bloodbath period. Led by Randy Robins before becoming an autonomous division, they played a significant role in history as a nomadic expedition party, which allowed them to trade valuable goods. |-| Corus Union = === Status: Semi-active === The Corus Union is an autonomous division that emerged after the Korthie Casiagen War, alongside the Azure Collective. This group has established undisclosed holdings in the Main Nine, where they focus on extracting the abundant [[Object 1|Almond water]] found in these areas. While they maintain separate relations, the Corus Union primarily remains neutral and largely uncontacted by most other factions, even within their Colonist peers. However, they have extensive connections with Almond Water Inc. and D.E.E.P. As a result, many [[Object 1|Almond water]] traders prefer to set up operations in Corus holdings, knowing they can secure agreements to utilize the higher-yield almond water levels, such as [[Level 4]], over other sources. |-| Osmi Association = === Status: Undisclosed === The Osmi Association was the last group to be acquired after extensive negotiations during the reformation. It serves as a powerful economic ally, helping the Colonists regain their footing. The discussions emphasized the importance of ensuring the survival of the collection of artifacts within the Main Nine. The Osmi Republic is particularly valuable due to its comprehensive archive system and its extensive network of outposts, which have largely been integrated into joint holdings. |-| E.N.T. Foundation = === Status: Active === The E.N.T. Foundation was originally established on Levels 1 and 4, with the primary goal of exterminating hostile entities. However, the group faced significant challenges during [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]], which created divisions over whether to operate as an open organization or maintain an elite force. Amid this turmoil, the group leader, Ben Firia, met with Colonist member Davies Auras, who was overseeing expedition teams in the area. Seeking unity, they decided to reform the foundation. With its members exhausted from internal conflict, elections were held, and the foundation ultimately joined the Colonists as a reformed divisional group. The E.N.T. Foundation's expertise in dealing with hostile entities proved invaluable in reaching out to other groups and combating entity outbreaks. |-| Partygoer Extinction Army = === Status: Dormant === As the oldest autonomous division within the Backroom Colonists, this group was united under the Unity Pact before the Bloodbath of Cluster 1. They were the first to negotiate their conditions for freedom and autonomy, playing a crucial role in establishing this system. Although the P.E.A. was a significant force during its time, the faction experienced a decline following the Euphoric Wars. This conflict led them to shift from an expansionist and highly militarized approach to a more peaceful and neutral stance, similar to that of the Colonists. == History ==
NOTICE: This section encourages user participation in updating lore, since lore is constantly evolving. However, users should follow the time system's logic and avoid referencing the FTD system.
Following guidelines implemented by the Collective Database, the history of the Backroom Colonists has been migrated to a separate page. The Database wants to show an unbiased view on both sides, with supporting evidence and files of all factions added in for further information. Records will be released to the public after further organization.

[[File:Backroom colonists member 3.png|center|600x600px|link=]]

''Colonist members playing the anthem by guitar.''
== Anthem == After losing the Great War, Minister Izumo Kodona announced the composition of an anthem, signaling a fresh start for the colony. The anthem, titled "The Children of Reality," was composed by the esteemed Vincent Maxfield Frank, affectionately known as Vini by the Colonists. The song serves as a reminder to the wanderers that, despite the hardships they have faced, coming together and striving for a brighter future is what truly matters. It symbolizes a phoenix rising from the ashes, working towards a better tomorrow.
[[File:Children of Reality (Backroom Colonists anthem, composed by Vini).mp3|500px]]
== Military == The Backroom Colonists are currently subject to limitations imposed by the demilitarization efforts following the post-Bloodbath surrender. Nonetheless, their substantial manpower and advanced technological capabilities, coupled with firsthand experience concerning the complexities of Backrooms combat, make them a formidable force. To this day, the Colonists still hold a strong influence among the known colonies, as evidenced by their involvement in the Euphoric Wars and their support during the Korthie Casiagen conflict. Presently, the Colonists maintain a military personnel count of 26,800, which encompasses security staff deployed at Colonist outposts as well as reserve forces poised for mobilization at the capital. Although military restrictions have historically been stringent, these limitations have begun to relax in light of improving relations with factions such as the [[M.E.G.]] In response to the restrictions imposed by demilitarization, the Colonists have established ten specialized division task forces aimed at enhancing quality over quantity. These task forces are highly adaptable and address a wide variety of potential challenges and contingencies. Each unit has a distinct identity, characterized by its unique history, traditions, and other elements shaped by its particular experiences and encounters within the Backrooms. |-| CORDELIA = === Legion I - Cordelia === This division is composed of various former groups that merged to form the inaugural Colonist task force. They have engaged in numerous conflicts encountered by the Colonists and have adeptly navigated through a variety of situations. By combining the "Children of Deadzone" alongside highly experienced crews and mercenaries, this division represents an elite yet chaotic force of destruction, unhesitant in executing the more arduous assignments. |-| ALDER = === Legion II - Alder === This veteran unit earned its name and formed into a special task force after achieving victory in a skirmish known as the "Battle of Alder Post." This division has extensive combat experience, primarily dealing with insurgent groups and terrorists. |-| EMBER = === Legion III - Ember === Considered to be the wild card of all divisions, and formed relatively early in the faction's history, this legion employs unique tactics, including scorched-earth strategies that saved the division during the Euphoric wars, earning it the title of "Ember". |-| FOXTROT = === Legion IV - Foxtrot === Known for their impressive battle record, the Foxtrot Legion incorporates unique initiation and morale-boosting tactics. Throughout its history, this legion and its subdivisions, sub-legions, have consistently proven effective in combat, especially when it comes to wide-scale entity eradication. |-| GEMINI = === Legion V - Gemini === Composed primarily of prior [[Aurielle]] members of the Colony of Geminia (former autonomous division), Gemini leverages the knowledge and skills acquired from the Aurielle conflicts. Going back to their roots, Gemini have recently partnered with the Republic of [[Aurielle]] for various training missions. |-| LUNA = === Legion VI - Luna === Stationed throughout the Backrooms during the Flicker, Luna has garnered names such as 'Sunrise' for its efforts to relight the Backrooms while upholding moral principles. Unlike other groups, Luna's ranks include specialists such as consulars and diplomats. This blend of pragmatism and principle defines their mission. |-| SYNAPSE = === Legion VII - Synapse === As the rapid response and technological unit of the Colonists, Synapse utilizes a variety of devices, equipment, and vehicles that enhance their covert missions—offering capabilities that other groups lack. |-| CONDOR = === Legion VIII - Condor === Renowned for its level of exploration, this legion comprises former expedition crews that petitioned for military benefits. Formed during Izumo Kodona's term, Condor continues to serve as a clearing and expediting force for the Colonists. |-| LUOBU = === Legion IX - Luobu === Luobu, the newest division established after Flicker, comprises various training divisions and functions as a reinforcement unit. It is known for collaborating with other independent divisions in its operations while providing essential support and intelligence to said divisions.

== Accomplishments == === Greek Classification === As the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System]] for levels was being rolled out, the Backroom Colonists found that several levels had not been classified yet. Due to fears that if they were assigned a number, the lack of communication between the Backroom Colonists and the [[M.E.G.]] would mean that another level had already been assigned to that spot, they were given Greek letters. Levels such as the [[The Delta Mall]] are examples of such situation, discovered and labeled by the Colonists. This naming system was later removed after the widespread implementation/usage of the Collective Database (now commonly referred to as the Database). === Objective Normative Ensemble (O.N.E.) === Objective Normative Ensemble, also known as O.N.E., is an autonomous logic model designed to serve as an nigh-sentient arbitral framework for the Arche database, ensuring that debates are grounded in foundational law and logical consistency. The model was developed using a combination of anomalous backrooms technologies and non-anomalous baseline reality components. Unlike standard language models, O.N.E. does not rely solely on pattern recognition; it incorporates several layers of logic within its code. This allows it to categorize millions of situations, build upon those categories to recognize/filter new information, while applying different layers of logic based on each category. O.N.E. also includes data servers that maintain integrated, contextual memories. This allows the model to recall nuanced opinions from all previous interactions while applying the necessary context to said memories. As a result, it can learn from these interactions and form its own ideas on various topics. Additionally, a switch can enable O.N.E. to have real-time, continuous learning capabilities for a set period. In this mode, it is equipped to register and interpret real-world situations, rather than relying solely on pre-existing data and updates. However, this requires significant power and can only be activated during critical moments. At other times, O.N.E. operates in a frozen state, maintaining its capabilities but not reacting to real-time situations like traditional language models. Despite this, it still retains a higher level of logic and reasoning and is typically devoid of AI hallucinations. === Almond Purifier === The Almond Purifier is a distinctive device designed specifically for filtering Almond Water. This bottle is equipped with advanced filters and has the capability to modify flavors, allowing Almond Water to taste like raspberry, apple, or cherry. === BackOS Navigator === Utilizing [[Phenomenon 2]], expedition teams can download the anomalous retro-futuristic BackOS system onto a custom-made device developed by the Colonists during the reformation period. By harnessing the powerful computing capabilities of this advanced technology, the device maps its surrounding environment as the user walks and attempts to match it with previously mapped areas when connected to [[Phenomenon 2]]. While it cannot guarantee perfect navigation due to the Backrooms’ anomalous, shifting, and non-Euclidean nature, it still assists in general direction and approximate readings of level conditions, as well as potential shifts in specific areas based on prior data. As a result, this device has become essential for expedition teams, aiding in navigation and helping them find their way back. === Entity Radar === A tripod-like device that functions as a radar system. The Entity Radar sends out 400 MHz and 300 GHz wavelengths every minute to detect whether that wave is disrupted by moving targets within its range. This allows the Colonists to identify the presence of lifeforms, or at least moving objects, within the device's radius. However, the accuracy of this detection decreases as distance increases, with a maximum effective range of 400 meters. Furthermore, by analyzing movement patterns, the device can estimate the types and numbers of entities present. Despite its bulkiness and stationary nature, the Entity Radar has improved the overall safety of establishing outposts.

[[File:Quantum messaging 2 (Backroom Colonists).webp|center|600x600px|link=]]

''A photograph of a grade 2 receiver.''
=== Quantum Messaging === {{Blurimage|file=Quantum messaging (Backroom Colonists).jpg|caption=Photograph taken on [[Level 37|Level 37]].}} Quantum messaging is a highly confidential technology pivotal to the development and expansion of the Colonists. Reversed engineered on [[Level 15]] by Italian theoretical physicist Ettore Majorana, the Colonists managed to grasp the fundamentals of advanced quantum tunneling and create a device that enables inter-dimensional communication. Rumored to run on neutrino-based technology, this allowed encoded messages to travel on quantum wavelengths and sub-light speed. While the date of creation is unknown, as there is no definitive way of tracking time in the backrooms, it is reasonable to say that the device was created before the Bloodbath of Cluster 1, since the Colonists were widely employing its use at the time. A central transmitter referred to as the Quantum Nexus is responsible for receiving, transferring, and connecting all other receivers to Base Arche. *'''Grade 1 receiver:''' A device given to lower positions, and can only receive messages from the base and send SOS signals. The build is rather simple and cannot be reverse-engineered since the fundamental logic runs from the central transmitter. *'''Grade 2 receiver:''' A device for high commands or members on special missions. This receiver can receive messages at a faster pace and also reply to them via text or real-time voice connection. There are intricate workings inside a grade 2 receiver, making the Colonists wary of employing it due to its high value and intricate workings. *'''Grade 3 receiver:''' Also known as top grade receiver, is the most complex messaging device developed by the Colonists; this device is barely employed and highly confidential. It is rumored that this device has a much stronger connection than the previous two receivers and can take/project holographic images. The only confirmed usages of this device were in the Bloodbath of Cluster 1, the terraforming of [[Level 94]] and the messaging attempts with Base Morpheus on [[Level 514]]. == Outposts == === Capital === The current capital of the Backroom Colonists is located on a covert level, codenamed Base Arche and affectionately referred to as New Almond City by its residents. Containing the largest population among the Colonists by far, New Almond City also houses several key systems within the colony, such as the Quantum Nexus, the Objective Normative Ensemble, and the Arche Parliament.
NOTICE: This section is open for user participation. Levels are constantly changing and users are encouraged to help expand it. Please update what levels contain Colonist outposts.
